Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

commit.message:
Add antigen-driven CD8 clonal expansion and Sorin et al. validation

- Add cd8_kill_prolif_prob parameter: CD8 divides into cleared grid square
  after killing tumor cell (literature-based p=0.5, not SBI-fitted)
- Validate CD8:Treg ratio as ICB biomarker using Sorin et al. 2025 DSP-GeoMx
  data (n=58, stromal CD8:Treg ratio p=0.012, Treg fraction p=0.001)

  low-CD8 patients (0% vs 88% clinical) — kill-prolif mechanism addresses this
- Add --kill-prolif CLI arg to human_luad_sweep.py
- Add SLURM script for kill-prolif sweep (H7)

commit.message:
feat: implement ConsistentHashRing (Phase 1 - Hash Containers)

ConsistentHashRing is a distributed hash table implementation with minimal
key redistribution when nodes are added or removed.

Features:
- Virtual nodes (replicas) for better load distribution
- Sorted ring with binary search for O(log n) lookup
- Minimal key migration on topology changes (~1/n keys remapped)
- Configurable replica count per physical node

Time Complexity:
- addNode: O(r log n) where r = replicas, n = total virtual nodes
- removeNode: O(r log n)
- getNode: O(log n) binary search

Tests: 17 passing
- Basic operations (init, add, remove, getNode)
- Empty ring handling
- Key redistribution verification (20-45% remapped on add)
- Iterator over unique physical nodes
- Clone functionality
- Ring sorting invariant validation
- High replica count (100 replicas)
- Stress test with 50 nodes
- Load distribution balance (±100 keys per node with 20 replicas)
- Memory leak detection

Phase 1 Hash Containers: COMPLETED (4/4) ✅

commit.message:
fix(ci): add allowed_tools for claude-code-review (#42)

## Summary
- Add `allowed_tools` to grant Claude Code permission to use `git`,
`gh`, `Read`, `Glob`, and `Grep` in CI — fixes the issue where the
code-review plugin couldn't post review comments due to 6 permission
denials per run
- Add `show_full_output: true` temporarily to diagnose any remaining
issues in CI logs

## Root Cause
Claude Code in CI runs non-interactively. Without explicit
`allowed_tools`, every tool call triggers a permission prompt that is
auto-denied. The `code-review` plugin needs `gh` to post PR comments and
`git`/`Read`/`Grep` to analyze diffs.

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify this PR itself triggers a claude-code-review run with 0
permission denials
- [ ] Verify Claude posts review comments on this PR
- [ ] After confirming, remove `show_full_output: true` in a follow-up

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
* Broadened when automated reviews run by removing optional PR and path
filters.
* Adjusted checkout to fetch full repository history for more complete
analysis.
* Expanded the review prompt to a multi-line format with clearer
guidance and post-review publishing instructions.
* Increased execution permissions to allow posting review comments and
related API actions.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

Add condition-based auto bet switching with 5-year backtest ROI

Based on 600-race 5-year backtest results (2020-2025):
- Condition A (8-14h/1600m+/good): Trio 7-bet, ROI 86.4%, HIT 42.2%
- Condition B (8-14h/1600m+/heavy): Wide 1ax-2flow, ROI 119.8%
- Condition C (15h+/1600m+/good): Wide 1ax-2flow, ROI 83.0%
- Condition D (sprint <=1400m): Not recommended (low hit rate)
- Condition E (<=7 horses): Umaren 1ax-2flow, ROI 142.5%
- ROI >= 80% conditions only show buy recommendation

Also adds:
- Condition-specific ROI table in 5-year backtest report
- Yearly ROI bar chart (Trio + Wide) with 100% reference line
- generate_umaren_bets() and generate_bets_for_condition()
- DB columns: bet_condition, bet_type, umaren_bets

commit.message:
Fix Google Drive octet-stream: add magic byte sniffing for file type detection

Google Drive download endpoint often returns Content-Type: application/octet-stream
instead of the real MIME type. Instead of rejecting it, we now:

1. Download the file buffer immediately when content-type is octet-stream
2. Sniff the real type from magic bytes:
   - %PDF header → application/pdf
   - PK\x03\x04 (ZIP) → PPTX or XLSX based on Content-Disposition filename
   - \x89PNG → image/png
   - \xFF\xD8 → image/jpeg
   - Falls back to Content-Disposition filename extension
3. Apply same sniffing to retried Google Drive responses (after HTML confirmation page)
4. Extracted saveBuffer() helper to remove code duplication

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clarify 0.2n savings attribution and upper bound limitations

Honestly explain that our derived upper bound is too loose to
quantify delay savings directly. The 0.2n improvement comes
from the commit's cited literature (K=1.207 vs K=1.0 average).

commit.message:
Classify ConcatAndPatternEquivalent → §10.10.1 [skip ci]

The test assigns an int[1:3] array using both unpacked array concatenation ({1,2,3}) and array assignment pattern ('{1,2,3}), then asserts element-wise equality. §10.10.1 "Unpacked array concatenations compared with array assignment patterns" defines exactly this equivalence and uses int A3[1:3]; A3={1,2,3}; A3='{1,2,3}; as its canonical example on LRM p.265.

Add "New Worktree Session" context menu action

Right-click any session node → "New Worktree Session..." opens the
existing wizard pre-configured for git worktree mode:

- Source repo pre-filled from the session's working directory
- Git mode auto-set to "Worktree"
- Git state detected automatically
- Prompt field focused — user types task description
- Branch name auto-inferred from the prompt (existing wizard logic)
- Worktree created as sibling directory, new Claude session launched

New API: ClaudeSessionWizard::setWorktreeSource(sourceDir)
New signal: SessionManagerPanel::worktreeSessionRequested(sourceDir)
MainWindow handles the signal chain: wizard → git worktree add → session

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify UnpackedArrayConcatDescending → §10.10 [skip ci]

§10.10 defines unpacked array concatenation and states that elements are arranged left-to-right to form the resulting array value. The test assigns `{10, 20, 30}` to `int arr [2:0]` (a descending-range unpacked array) and verifies that element 0 gets 30, element 1 gets 20, and element 2 gets 10 — exactly the ordering behaviour governed by §10.10. There is no finer subclause within 10.10 in the 1800-2023 LRM.

commit.message:
feat(sentry): enable Session Replay for iOS 17-25 (AMA-1084) (#101)

* feat(sentry): enable Session Replay for iOS 17-25 users (AMA-1084)

Adds Session Replay to SentrySDK.start:
- sessionSampleRate = 0.1  (10% background capture, low quota impact)
- onErrorSampleRate = 1.0  (100% capture on crashes and hangs)

SDK is already at 8.57.3 which auto-disables replay on iOS 26+/Xcode 26+
(Liquid Glass masking issue) — no manual guard needed.

Default masking covers all text, images, and user input. No custom
masking config required — confirmed no custom drawing in any view.

Resolves AMA-1084

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(sentry): use options.sessionReplay not options.experimental.sessionReplay

In sentry-cocoa 8.57.3, sessionReplay was graduated from experimental
and is now a top-level property on SentryOptions. The old path caused
a compile error on CI.

Resolves AMA-1084

* fix: add explicit self.pendingQueueKey for Swift 6 strict concurrency

Xcode 26 / Swift 6 requires explicit self. when referencing stored
properties in certain closure contexts. This was a latent issue that
surfaces on full recompile with the new toolchain.

* ci: trigger PR tests

* fix: update session replay comment (trigger CI)

* fix: use [:] empty dict literal and self.storageKey in Swift 6 closure (PersonalDictionary)

---------

commit.message:
fix: relax above-barre spread for standard G, add openGaps penalty

- Above-barre spread check now allows wide string spread when fret
  difference is small (≤2 frets). Fixes G chord 320003 being rejected.
- Track openGaps: muted strings adjacent to open strings = fingerpicking
  only technique. Apply -40 penalty in scoring.
- Open string bonus now uses sliding scale based on avgFret position.
  Pure open chords get full bonus, high fret + open combos get reduced.
